intended_use: BriefCase is a radiological computer aided triage and notification software indicated for use in the analysis of CTPA images in adults or transitional adolescents aged 18 and older. The device is inte
intended_use_full: BriefCase is a radiological computer aided triage and notification software indicated for use in the analysis of CTPA images in adults or transitional adolescents aged 18 and older. The device is intended to assist hospital networks and appropriately trained medical specialists in workflow triage by flagging and communication of suspected positive findings of Pulmonary Embolism (PE) pathologies.
ground_truth: Ground truth determined by three senior board-certified radiologists
other_metrics: Positive Likelihood Ratio (PLR): 15.903; Negative Likelihood Ratio (NLR): 0.055; Time-to-notification: 78.0 seconds (95% CI: 73.6-82.3); Additional Operating Point 1 (AOP1): Sensitivity 98.60%, Specificity 85.26%; Additional Operating Point 2 (AOP2): Sensitivity 86.45%, Specificity 98.25%
